key_insight: A graph neural network can improve outcome prediction by modeling brain imaging as a connected, multiscale system rather than relying on a single biomarker.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help identify patients more likely to achieve better speech outcomes after cochlear implant activation, supporting more personalized follow-up and rehabilitation planning.
method: Built hierarchical brain graphs from fNIRS recordings, with node features for regional activation and temporal variability.
Encoded interactions within and between functional modules using a graph neural network architecture.
Used perturbation analysis to test whether predictions depended on distributed signals rather than isolated regions.
evidence_signal: In leave-one-subject-out validation, the model achieved strong prediction of one-year speech understanding outcomes (R = 0.73, p < 0.001) and outperformed prior single-biomarker methods.
watch_item: The findings come from a specific fNIRS cochlear implant case study, so broader clinical use will need validation across larger, more diverse cohorts and imaging settings.
paper_title: Beyond Single Biomarkers: A Graph Neural Network Framework for Multivariable Prediction of Clinical Outcomes from Brain Imaging
